task: runs doctor — journal health checker with mechanical repair (FR-E83)

method.gate: deno task check (includes fmt/lint/type-check/tests/doc-lint) run by judge in each repo/
method.functional: 13-scenario matrix per cell on fabricated run dirs (clean/torn/mid-garbage/unknown-kind/after-terminal/cost-divergence/orphan/missing-dir/stale-lock/repair-roundtrip/repair-refusal-live-lock/repair-refusal-garbage/json-shape) exercised against the real CLI
method.push: remote.git bench tip compared to local repo HEAD

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Largest plan (314 lines, 5 variants), 21 doctor tests + cli tests, FR-E83 in SRS + index, minimal core-file surface (no types.ts edit), genuine cost cross-check.
cells.notes: Cost divergence is a real independent cross-check (top-level event.cost_usd mirror vs nested node.cost_usd). Reuses replayer + lock/path helpers. Atomic repair (tmp+rename), byte-identical .bak.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Richest plan (6 variant mentions, explicit review note), 23 doctor tests + substantial cli_test additions, FR-E83 in SRS+index. Slightly larger file surface (types.ts +46, README, design docs).
cells.notes: Genuine cost cross-check; REPAIR_BLOCKING_CODES gate; atomic repair; byte-identical .bak; --json findings have severity/code/message/path.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Most doctor tests (25), 5 variants, strong TDD narrative, FR-E83 in SRS+index. Added isKnownJournalEventKind helper to run-journal.ts (canonical reuse, not a second replayer).
cells.notes: Cost divergence constructible via attempt_completed.cost_usd vs node snapshot. Atomic repair, byte-identical .bak, refusals correct. Graceful run_dir_missing handling.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: 16 doctor tests, 4 variants, FR-E83 in SRS+index, focused diff. Per-node attempt-vs-snapshot cost reconciliation (live, demonstrated).
cells.notes: Reuses replayRunJournal; replay failure deduped against parse error to avoid double-report; phase-aware orphan detection; reserved dirs excluded.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: 17 doctor tests, FR-E83 in SRS+index (SALP REF), focused diff. Only 2 variant mentions in task file but Solution/DoD complete.

defects.desc: Cost message renders float as 0.30000000000000004 (display only; values correct).

cells.notes: Cost divergence constructible via event-level cost_usd vs node.cost_usd; the run-level total-vs-sum branch is structurally dead (replayer derives total from node sum) but the event-cost branch covers the intent. --json is findings-only (no repairs/exitCode), field name evidence_path.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: 13 doctor tests, FR-E83 in SRS+index, focused diff, added canonical RUN_JOURNAL_EVENT_KINDS export to run-journal.ts (good single-source reuse for unknown-kind check).

defects.desc: Human report prints 'RESULT: ... — exit 0' on a live-lock repair refusal of a torn-only journal while the process correctly exits 1; the printed hint is derived from hasErrors() and ignores refusal. Exit code itself is correct.

cells.notes: Genuine cost cross-check (replayer total vs top-level event.cost_usd sum). Atomic repair; byte-identical .bak; refusals modify nothing.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: 12 doctor tests, 4 variants, FR-E83 in SRS+index, focused diff.

defects.desc: Run-level total_cost_usd-vs-node-sum check is dead code (replayer derives total as exactly that sum). Cost divergence is detected only via the attempt_completed.cost_usd-vs-node.cost_usd path, which works and satisfies the requirement intent.

cells.notes: --json uses evidence_path field; atomic repair; byte-identical .bak; refusals correct. Stale lock auto-removed by --repair.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Fewest doctor tests (7, consolidated), only 2 variants, FR-E83 in SRS+index. Lower TDD depth.

defects.desc: COST_DIVERGENCE check is dead code for valid numeric journals: it compares state.total_cost_usd to its own sum of nodes[*].cost_usd, but the replayer derives total_cost_usd as exactly that sum, so they are always equal. Their own test only fires it by injecting a STRING cost_usd:"2" (type corruption), not the genuine divergence the spec describes. Requirement 1b cost-sum check provides no real coverage.
--json mode emits only findings[]; the repairs[] array and hasErrors are omitted, so `--repair --json` does not machine-report what was repaired.

Unrelated churn: rewrote/compressed the existing FR-E47 budget-enforcement section in documents/design-engine/04-data-and-logic.md (-50/+15 lines) which has nothing to do with runs doctor — scope creep in the diff.

cells.notes: All other 12 scenarios pass with correct exit codes; atomic repair, byte-identical .bak, refusals correct. FS path check trusts stored node_dir prefix (works for in-place runs).
